---
title: Linear Differential Equations
description: First-order linear differential equations.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Recognize the form of a first-order linear ODE.
- Interpret forcing and decay terms.
- Prepare for numerical solution methods.
:::

First-order linear equations have the form:

```text
dy/dx + p(x)y = q(x)
```

::: {.practice-box}
1. Identify `p(x)` and `q(x)` in `dy/dx + 2y = x`.
2. What does the term involving `y` often represent?
3. Why are linear equations easier than nonlinear ones?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: `p(x) = 2` and `q(x) = x`.

Compare `dy/dx + 2y = x` with `dy/dx + p(x)y = q(x)`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: feedback, decay, growth, or resistance proportional to the current
state.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: linear structure supports systematic solution methods and avoids many
of the complications caused by products or powers of the unknown function.
:::
